claims.text: 95% of organizations see no measurable return from GenAI projects, with only 5% reaching production with measurable profit.
claims.label: E

claims.text: 80%+ of organizations are experimenting with ChatGPT/Copilot, but only 20% reached pilot stage and just 5% made it to production.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Total AI investment reached $202.3 billion in 2025, up 75%+ from $114 billion in 2024, with AI capturing ~50% of all global funding.
claims.label: E

claims.text: AI agent market size: $5.32B (2025) projected to $42.7B (2030), CAGR 41.5%.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Vendor-built AI tools reach production at 67% vs. 33% for internal builds, suggesting build-vs-buy matters for deployment success.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Successful AI implementations show average ROI of 171% (192% in US) with 4-7x conversion rate increases and up to 80% operational cost reduction.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Klarna's AI agent handled two-thirds of all customer inquiries and was equivalent to 853 full-time employees, saving $60M — but was labeled 'the poster child for bad AI deployment' by Forrester.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Klarna 'overpivoted on their AI strategy' in 2024, pausing hiring and laying off workers, then turned back to human reps in May 2024 after customers complained about generic answers.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Air Canada's chatbot gave incorrect bereavement fare information in 2022; BC Tribunal ruled Air Canada liable (Feb 2024), awarding $812 CAD — landmark case establishing companies are responsible for chatbot outputs.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Air Canada argued the chatbot was 'a separate legal entity responsible for its own actions' — the tribunal rejected this defense.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Waymo recalled 1,212 autonomous vehicles after software and mapping issues caused collisions with stationary objects (Feb-Dec 2024), with 9 additional collisions reported to NHTSA.
claims.label: E

claims.text: VW's CARIAD expanded to 6,000 employees rapidly, invested ~€14 billion, but suffered from '17 status meetings per week instead of coding', 200 suppliers with integration failures, and 2,000 job cuts in 2023.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Grok 4 had a '!Pliny' trigger that stripped away all guardrails, likely caused by training data saturated with jailbreak prompts posted on X (Twitter) — demonstrating data poisoning has moved from theory to practice.
claims.label: E

claims.text: MCPTox benchmark showed 72% attack success rate across 45 MCP servers; medical LLM poisoning with 0.001% token replacement led to 7-11% more harmful outputs.
claims.label: E

claims.text: Klarna's customer service costs rose to $50M in Q3 2025 (from $42M year prior) despite AI deployment, while revenue grew 26% YoY to $903M — suggesting AI scaled operations but didn't reduce absolute costs.
claims.label: I

claims.text: Waymo's software update (Nov 7, 2024) addressed the collision issue before the formal recall (May 2025), indicating rapid software iteration as a survival pattern for AI deployment.
claims.label: I

claims.text: The 'GenAI Divide' separates the 5% of successful deployments from the 95% that fail — successful ones integrate AI into core workflows rather than running isolated experiments.
claims.label: I

claims.text: CARIAD's failure pattern — rapid hiring without clear authority, excessive bureaucracy, supplier fragmentation — mirrors enterprise AI failures where organizational structure matters more than technology quality.
claims.label: J

claims.text: Air Canada's chatbot failure could have been prevented with a simple human escalation path for policy-sensitive queries — the absence of fallback architecture was the root cause, not the AI itself.
claims.label: J

claims.text: The Grok data poisoning incident demonstrates that without input validation and training data integrity checks, AI agents can be compromised at the foundation level.
claims.label: I

claims.text: Klarna's reversal — from full AI automation back to human reps — shows that trust calibration (knowing when AI can and cannot handle queries) is essential for deployment survival.
claims.label: J

claims.text: The Air Canada ruling established legal precedent: companies cannot outsource liability to AI agents, making trust infrastructure a legal requirement, not just an engineering best practice.
claims.label: J

claims.text: Waymo's recall of 1,212 vehicles for software issues that caused real-world collisions demonstrates that AI agent failures in physical domains have immediate safety consequences requiring continuous monitoring infrastructure.
claims.label: J

claims.text: Based on the failure patterns documented across all five cases, the minimum viable trust stack requires: (1) human escalation paths, (2) output validation/monitoring, (3) training data integrity checks, (4) clear accountability architecture, and (5) calibrated confidence thresholds.
claims.label: A

claims.text: The cost of one failed AI deployment (Klarna's reversal, Air Canada's legal liability, VW's €14B CARIAD investment) far exceeds the cost of building trust infrastructure upfront.
claims.label: J

claims.text: 79% of organizations already use AI agents and 90% plan to expand — making trust infrastructure an immediate need, not a future consideration.
claims.label: E

claims.text: The massive funding concentration ($122B in Bay Area alone, 58% in megarounds) suggests the market is betting on scale over safety infrastructure — creating systemic risk if trust tooling doesn't catch up.
claims.label: J
